response.variantDescriptions: {"variant": {"default": "A standard informative message, often used for general notifications or neutral updates.", "destructive": "Indicates a critical error, a dangerous action, or an issue that requires immediate attention and might prevent user progress.", "success": "Confirms that an action was completed successfully or that a positive state has been achieved.", "warning": "Alerts the user to a non-critical issue, a potential problem, or an action that might have unintended consequences."}}
response.description: The Alert component is a crucial UI element designed to capture user attention and communicate important, time-sensitive information. It serves as a non-dismissible callout box that displays various types of messages, including informational notes, warnings, errors, or success confirmations. Key features include support for different visual `variant` types, allowing for clear differentiation of message urgency and type (e.g., a red background for errors, green for success). It often incorporates optional icon support to enhance visual communication. Common use cases include providing feedback after user actions, notifying users of system status changes, highlighting critical issues that require immediate attention, or confirming the successful completion of a process.

guidance.whenToUse: Use the Alert component to display critical, time-sensitive, or important information that requires immediate user attention. This includes error messages, warning messages, success confirmations, or general informational notices that are crucial for the user's current task or understanding of the system state. It is ideal for non-modal communication that should remain visible until the user addresses the underlying issue or navigates away.
guidance.accessibility: Ensure the Alert component uses the appropriate ARIA role, such as `role='alert'` for dynamic, important, and time-sensitive information that is not user-dismissible, or `role='status'` for less urgent updates. If the alert contains interactive elements or requires user action, consider `role='alertdialog'` and manage focus appropriately. The message content should be clear, concise, and understandable. Provide sufficient color contrast for text and background combinations for all variants. For icon-supported alerts, ensure the icon is either purely decorative or has appropriate accessible text if it conveys unique meaning not present in the main message.
guidance.whenNotToUse: Do not use the Alert component for trivial information that doesn't require immediate attention, or for general page content. Avoid using it for interactive elements or as a replacement for form validation messages that appear next to specific input fields. For temporary, dismissible notifications that don't block user flow, consider a 'Toast' component instead. For complex, interactive messages that require a user decision, a 'Dialog' or 'Modal' component might be more appropriate. Overuse of alerts can lead to user fatigue and diminish their impact.

common.code:
<Alert variant="default">
  <AlertTitle>Information</AlertTitle>
  <AlertDescription>
    Your session will expire in 5 minutes. Please save your work.
  </AlertDescription>
</Alert>
common.title: Default Alert
common.description: An informational alert with the default styling.
An alert indicating a critical error or a dangerous action.
common.code:
<Alert variant="destructive">
  <AlertTitle>Error</AlertTitle>
  <AlertDescription>
    Unable to process your request. Please try again later.
  </AlertDescription>
</Alert>
common.title: Destructive Alert (Error)

common.code:
<Alert variant="success">
  <AlertTitle>Success!</AlertTitle>
  <AlertDescription>
    Your changes have been saved successfully.
  </AlertDescription>
</Alert>
common.title: Success Alert
common.description: An alert confirming a successful operation.
An alert indicating a non-critical issue or a potential problem.
common.title: Warning Alert
common.code:
<Alert variant="warning">
  <AlertTitle>Warning</AlertTitle>
  <AlertDescription>
    Some features may not be available in your current region.
  </AlertDescription>
</Alert>

minimal.description: The simplest working example of an Alert.
minimal.code:
<Alert>
  <AlertTitle>Heads up!</AlertTitle>
  <AlertDescription>
    You can add components to your app using the cli.
  </AlertDescription>
</Alert>
minimal.title: Basic Alert
